front: How do Pub/Sub dead letter topics work and what retry policies are available?
back: A dead letter topic (DLT) is a separate Pub/Sub topic that receives messages a subscription fails to process after a configured maximum delivery attempt count (5 to 100). After the threshold is reached, Pub/Sub automatically forwards the message to the DLT and stops redelivering it to the original subscription. Retry policies control the backoff between attempts: either immediate retry or exponential backoff with configurable min and max delay. DLTs enable error isolation without blocking healthy messages.
